如今，在北半球，春天开始于3月21日开始，当白昼和夜间的长度相等时，这对应于一只昼夜平行。中世纪作者太关联了春天的开始。在他七世纪百科的百科全书中，塞维利亚的Isidore写道，3月份是“也称新事物的月份，因为3月份是年初。它也被称为从萌发的迹象称为“新春”......“但是伊西多尔为什么符合3月份作为”年初“，你可能想知道？在中世纪欧洲的几个地区，民用日历遵循了通告模型，在盛宴日变化。
 因此，在通告式日历中，3月24日是一年中的最后一天，3月25日标志着新年的开始。通告是3月25日的基督徒假期，庆祝Archangel Gabriel对圣母玛利亚的访问，当时他告诉她她会成为耶稣基督的母亲上帝的儿子。因此，新的日历年开始春天，一个与新生活相关的赛季。这种强大的春天和重生之间的关联是宗教（通告）和经验，即基于观察（带春天来新的叶子，草和迁徙物种的回报）。
  中世纪的人是如何感知春天的到来？ Spring是如何在中世纪日历和文学文本中描绘的？而且，春天为人类的“交配季节”，因为它是许多动物物种？
   可以说，这一年的司进入四个明确的季节，每个季节与特定的发生日期都是人为的。当地气候影响 - 仍然影响 - 个人经验和对季节的看法。在西伯利亚，冬季在3月底突然结束;虽然，同时，在地球的温暖地区，春天已经在进行中。无论是到达的那一年，春天，塞维利亚的伊雷尔在拉丁文中解释了它的名字“因为它”是绿色的'（Venere），然后，在冬天之后，地球穿着植物和一切爆发进花。“
 在古典日历中，春天在5月初开始于2月和夏天。原因是天文和经验。在古典时代和希腊罗马视角，世界中心是温暖的地中海盆地，农作物比北方更早发芽。然而，在十二世纪和十三世纪，欧洲作家修改了古典日历，以使其适应其基督教文化和北极气候现实。在古典日历中，昼夜平分区和索斯蒂斯在季节中间落下，但对于中世纪作者来说，他们标志着他们的开始。在北半球，春天的春天以来的中世纪以来。
  春季影响物理日历到达的地方差异。第十三世纪法国作家博瓦斯的文森特（Beauvais）例如，在北部纬度地区，葡萄藤的修剪应该在3月而不是2月份，因为南方农民所做的，因为气候较冷。农业活动的适当日期取决于当地背景和天气。但是，在西欧，艺术公约指导艺术家的工作，装饰着春天的春天与4月份的春天。在后期中世纪的日历页面插入了廉价的祈祷书籍，3月份出现在一个月内。
   在后期中世纪日历的三月页上，树木仍然贫瘠，但地面，以前呈褐色或雪覆盖着，是绿色的色调，而春天的第一个迹象表现出色。 3月份，农民在工作，通常修剪树木和藤蔓或抚育作物。 4月4日畅销春天的户外场景，沐浴在温暖的阳光下，盛开的花朵和树木装满了叶子。 
春天的景色经常出发了本月的劳动力，转向贵族 - 这种日历的实际所有者。 4月，艺术家经常描绘了一个追求一个女人的男人，为她挑选花朵，或从事户外活动的夫妻。可能有时可以随心所欲地与招传/户外场景或令人叹为观止的崇高春季休闲。从六月开始，场景搬回了农民和月份的劳动力。这些漫画场景相当普遍，并加强了春天与爱的季节之间的文学联系。
  “Reverdie”或“重新绿化”是一种文学类型，庆祝春天和爱情中高中世纪的宫廷诗歌。其传感器的惯例模糊不清，但Reverdie是其主题：春天和爱情。遐想庆祝了4月和5月，他们唤起了温暖的轻盈和令人愉快的天气，自然的美丽，叶子和鲜花，斯普林斯和鸟类的声音。即使是开放的曲柄坎特伯雷故事借给Reverdie类型，当他们唤起了4月风的“甜蜜的呼吸”，它的“甜蜜的”淋浴，“嫩射击”和“小家禽”唱着他们的“旋律”。遐想通常是贵族特色：诗人，骑士和女士;与日历页面上的相同，持有花或猎鹰。
 Reverdie的场景后来插入浪漫以打开故事或带来新的叙事发展。 Reverdie主题也突出地在另一个文学和抒情类型中出现了“Pastourelle”。山地讲述故事，通常在春天举办的骑士试图勾引牧羊犬，并且通常与她发生性关系，或者如果她拒绝，强奸她。在故事结束时，骑士经常逃脱。然后被认为是顽皮的或疯狂的或彻头彻尾的色情。一些历史学家认为，这种类型是为了应对宫廷诗歌，更加克制和面向同意的未作出联盟。山地队伍模仿宫廷文学，他们也借给了Reverdie并以“爱”故事。但为什么这种春天与爱情协会？春天也是人类的交配季节吗？
   晚期中世纪婚姻合同和嫁妆的分析表明，2月和3月在婚姻周期中标志着低潮。在英格兰拉姆西修道院，在第十四和第十五世纪末，在这两个月内给予了庄园的农民很少有婚姻许可证。但婚姻许可证的数量授予4月和5月。原因是借出的，基督徒的禁食时期，在此期间人们应该转向上帝，避免吃肉和性行为。神学家认为人们应该在借出和在出现期间和圣约翰节日前的三周内毫不结合。
  Lenten Marriages的问题联系了性别在基督教工会的有效性中的作用。要完全有效，配偶必须完善婚姻。但如果在借出期间结婚并尊重无性别统治的一对夫妇，他们的婚姻几乎没有站立。法国南部的婚姻合同表明那里也是，借出的婚姻是一个禁忌。在蒙彼利埃地区，婚姻季节在圣诞节和借出之间落下，即在1月份和2月初。几乎没有婚姻发生在2月中旬和3月，符合借出的限制。婚姻合同在春季期间在来源中卷土重来，但没有达到1月级别。
 因此，春天确实是婚姻的季节，至少与借出的限制相比。至于性别和生育，中世纪来源很少能够使历史学家能够精确地确定出生（和概念）发生的年度。然而，早期的现代文件授予这一特权。虽然他们在中世纪贴上了中世纪，但各个世纪以来的传统的永久性以及教会在影响私人事务方面的作用使一些假设能够。法国十七世纪，1月至4月之间出生的峰值，表明孩子们在4月至6月开始构思儿童，即在借出之后。在镜子效应中，诞生后出生率下降了九个月，这表明许多人尊重2月和3月盛行的无性别规则。
  中世纪的出生季节性可能遵循了许多人尊重的宗教处方的可比路径。春天确实是爱的季节，并标志着一个新的生活循环的开始。随着花朵盛开的，动物很交配，人们回到结婚和制作孩子。 
Lucie Laumonier是Concordia大学的联盟助理教授。 点击此处查看她的Academia.edu页面或在法国中世纪的Instagram上关注她。  Ardis Butterfield，中世纪法国的诗歌和音乐：从Jean Renart到Guillaume de Machaut（剑桥大学出版社，2002年）。  顶级图片：15世纪的春天描绘 - 英国图书馆Ms King的24个。 17.
note_en:
Nowadays, in the Northern hemisphere, Spring starts on March 21, which corresponds to an equinox, when daylight and nighttime are equal in length. Medieval authors too associated the beginning of Spring with March. In his seventh-century encyclopedia known as the  Etymologies, Isidore of Seville wrote that March is “also called the month of new things, because the month of March is the beginning of the year. It is also called the “new Spring” from its signs of germination…” But why did Isidore qualify March as “the beginning of the year”, you might wonder? In several regions of medieval Europe, civil calendars followed the Annunciation model, with a change of year on the Feast day.
 In Annunciation-style calendars, therefore, March 24 was the last day of the year, and March 25 marked the beginning of the new year. The Annunciation is a Christian holiday falling on March 25, that celebrates the archangel Gabriel’s visit to the Virgin Mary, when he told her she would be the mother of Jesus Christ the son of God. The new calendar year therefore began with Spring, a season linked to new life. This powerful association between Spring and rebirth was both religious (the Annunciation) and empirical, that is, based on observation (with Spring come new leaves, grass and the return of migratory species).
  How did medieval people perceive the arrival of Spring? How was Spring depicted in medieval calendars and literary texts? And, was Spring the “mating season” for humans, as it is for many animal species?
   Arguably, the division of the year into four well-defined seasons each associated with a specific date of occurrence is artificial. Local climate impacted – and still impacts – individual experiences and perceptions of seasons. In Siberia, Winter does not abruptly end at the end of March; while, at the same time, in warmer regions of the globe, Spring is already underway. Regardless of the month of the year it arrived, Spring, Isidore of Seville explained, in Latin  ver, got its name “because it ‘is green’ ( virere), for then, after winter, the earth is clothed with plants and everything bursts into flower.”
 In classical calendars, Spring began in February and Summer in early May. The reasons were astronomical and empirical. In classical times and from the Greek-Roman perspective, the centre of the world was the warm Mediterranean basin, where crops germinate earlier than in the North. By the twelfth and thirteenth century, however, European writers had modified the classical calendar to adapt it to their Christian culture and Northern climatic realities. In classical calendars, equinoxes and solstices fell in the middle of the seasons, but for medieval authors, they marked their beginning. In the Northern hemisphere, Spring springs in March since the Middle Ages.
  Local differences in the arrival of Spring influenced agrarian calendars. Thirteenth-century French author Vincent of Beauvais noted for instance that, in Northern latitudes, the pruning of vines should be done in March instead of in February, like southern farmers do, because of the colder climate. Appropriate dates for agrarian work depended on the local context and the weather. But, across Western Europe, artistic conventions guiding the work of artists adorning lavish calendars associated Spring with the month of April. On the late medieval calendar pages inserted inexpensive books of prayers, March appears as an in-between month.
   On the March page of late medieval calendars, trees still laid barren but the ground, formerly brownish or covered with snow, was painted in greener tones while the first signs of Spring manifested. In March, peasants were at work, usually pruning trees and vines or tending to their crops. April opened the Spring cycle with pleasant outdoor scenes, bathed in warmer sunlight with blooming flowers and trees full of leaves.
 Spring scenes often departed from the labours of the month to turn towards the nobility – the actual owners of such calendars. In April, artists often depicted a man courting a woman, picking flowers for her, or a couple engaged in an outdoorsy activity. The month of May sometimes kept going with a courting/outdoor scene or featured the noble Spring leisure of hawking. From June, the scenes moved back to the peasantry and the labours of the months. These courting scenes were fairly common and strengthened a literary association between Spring and the season of love.
  The “ reverdie,” or “re-greening” is a literary genre celebrating Spring and love that emerged in the courtly poetry of the High Middle Ages. Its stylistic conventions are vague but the  reverdie is featured by its themes: Spring and love. Reverdies celebrated the months of April and May, they evoked the warmer light and delightful weather, the beauty of nature, its leaves and flowers, the sound of springs and bird songs. Even the opening verses of Chaucer’s  Canterbury Tales borrow to the  reverdie genre when they evoke the “sweet breath” of April’s winds, its “sweet” showers, the “tender shoots” and the “small fowls” singing their “melody”. The reverdies usually featured noble characters: poets, knights and ladies; the same that appeared on the calendar pages, holding a flower or a falcon.
 Scenes of reverdie were later inserted in romances to open the story or to bring new narrative developments. The reverdie theme also appeared prominently in another literary and lyric genre called “ pastourelle.” Pastourelles tell the story, usually set in Springtime, of a knight attempting to seduce a shepherdess and, often, having sexual relations with her or, if she refused, raping her. At the end of the story, the knight often escaped. Pastourelles were then considered naughty or saucy, or downright erotic. Some historians hold that the genre developed in response to courtly poetry, more restrained and oriented toward consensual unconsummated unions. Pastourelles mimicked courtly literature insofar that they too borrowed to the reverdie and featured a “love” story. But why this association of Spring with Love? Was Spring the mating season for humans as well?
   The analysis of late medieval marriage contracts and dowry payments suggests that February and March marked a low ebb in the cycle of marriages. At the Ramsey Abbey, England, in the late fourteenth and fifteenth century, few marriage licences were given to peasants of the manor during those two months. But the number of marriage licences granted surged in April and in May. The reason was Lent, the Christian period of fasting, during which people should turn toward God, avoid eating meat and having sex. Theologians considered that people should refrain from marrying during Lent as well as during Advent, and during the three weeks before the Feast of St. John.
  The issue with Lenten marriages connects to the role of sex in the validity of Christian unions. To be fully valid, a marriage had to be consummated by the spouses. But if a couple married during Lent and respected the no-sex rule, their marriage barely stood. Marriages contracts from the South of France show that there too, marriages during Lent were a no-go. In the Montpellier area, the season of marriage fell between Christmas and Lent, that is in January and early February. Virtually no marriage occurred after mid-February and in March, in tune with the Lent restrictions. Marriage contracts made a comeback in sources during Spring, but without reaching the January level.
 Spring, therefore, was indeed the season of marriages, at least in contrast with the restrictions looming over Lent. As for sex and procreation, medieval sources rarely enable historians to precisely pinpoint the time of the year when births (and conception) occurred. Early modern documents however grant that privilege. Although they postdate the Middle Ages, the perpetuation of traditions across centuries and the role of the Church in influencing private matters enable some hypotheses. In seventeenth-century France, the peak of births occurred between January and April, indicating that the children were conceived the year prior, from April to June, that is, after Lent. In a mirror effect, births dropped nine months after Lent, suggesting that many respected the no-sex rule that prevailed in February and March.
  The seasonality of births in the Middle Ages likely followed a comparable path, framed by religious prescriptions that many people respected. Spring was indeed the season of love and marked the beginning of a new cycle of life. As flowers were blooming, animals were mating and people went back to marrying and making children.
 Lucie Laumonier is an Affiliate assistant professor at Concordia University.   Click here to view her Academia.edu page or follow her on Instagram at   The French Medievalist.
    Ardis Butterfield,    Poetry and Music in Medieval France: From Jean Renart to Guillaume de Machaut (Cambridge University Press, 2002).

title: 中世纪的春天

note:
今天早上，我们看着令人敬畏，因为第一个火星直升机，聪明，乘坐薄火星氛围。这对NASA和喷气机推进实验室（JPL）的团队来说是一个令人难以置信的成就。这也是来自世界各地的一个不可见的开源开发人员的不可见的团队，这也是一项成就。事实上，Github上的近12,000名开发人员通过开源贡献了Ingenuity的软件。然而，与黑洞的第一个图像一样，这些开发商的大多数甚至都不知道他们有助于使第一个火星直升机飞行成为可能。
  今天，我们希望使看不见的可见。因此，我们曾与JPL合作，将新的火星2020直升机使命徽章放在贡献的每个开发人员的Github配置文件上，他们为聪明才智使用的任何开源项目和库的特定版本做出了贡献。您可以查看JPL团队在此处使用的SCIPY，Linux和F Prime（F'）的完整项目列表。
  我们还使用这个机会向GitHub配置文件引入新的成就部分。现在，成就包括火星2020直升机使命徽章，北极代码金库徽章，以及通过Github赞助商赞助开源工作的徽章。关注此空间！
 阅读新徽章背后的故事以及开源贡献者如何帮助Ingenuity在Readme项目上飞行。
 祝贺NASA和JPL的团队，以及成千上万的开发人员，他们成为今天的第一个火星飞行。我们仍然在地球上，但你的代码现在在火星上！
note_en:
This morning, we watched in awe as the first Mars Helicopter,  Ingenuity, took flight in the thin Martian atmosphere. This is an incredible achievement for the teams at NASA and the  Jet Propulsion Lab (JPL). It’s also an achievement powered, in part, by an invisible team of open source developers from around the world. In fact, nearly 12,000 developers on GitHub contributed to Ingenuity’s software via open source. And yet, much like  the first image of a black hole, most of these developers are not even aware that they helped make the first Martian helicopter flight possible.
  Today, we want to make the invisible visible. So, we have worked with JPL to place a new Mars 2020 Helicopter Mission badge on the GitHub profile of every developer who contributed to the specific versions of any open source projects and libraries used by Ingenuity. You can check out the full list of projects like SciPy, Linux, and  F Prime (F’) that were used by the JPL team  here.
  We are also using this opportunity to introduce a new Achievements section to the GitHub profile. Right now, Achievements include the Mars 2020 Helicopter Mission badge, the  Arctic Code Vault badge, and a badge for sponsoring open source work via  GitHub Sponsors. Watch this space!
 Read the story behind the new badge and how open source contributors helped Ingenuity take flight on  The ReadME Project.
 Congratulations to the teams at NASA and JPL, and to the thousands of developers who made today’s first Martian flight possible. We’re all still here on Earth, but your code is now on Mars!
title: 开源进入火星

note:
波音747-400飞机于1988年首次推出，仍在通过3.5英寸软盘接收关键软件更新。登记册在航空公司决定在冠状病毒大流行期间坠入线之后退休后，笔测试合作伙伴的安全研究人员最近获得了英国航空公司747。该团队能够检查乘客甲板下方的完整航空电子湾，其数据中心样架的模块化黑匣子架，对飞机执行不同的功能。
 Pen Test Partners在驾驶舱中发现了3.5英寸的软盘驱动器，用于加载重要导航数据库。它是一个数据库，必须每28天更新一次，并且工程师每个月访问最新更新。
  虽然它可能听起来很令人惊讶的是，当今飞机仍在使用3.5英寸的软盘时，许多波音的737年也一直在使用软盘来加载航空电子软件多年。根据Aviation今天的2015年报告，根据航空的2015年报告，越来越大的数据库越来越多地变大。一些航空公司已经远离使用软盘，但其他航空公司陷入了每个月访问的工程师才能坐下来坐下来加载八个，并加载八个手册与机场，飞行路径，跑道等更新。
 747（上文）的10分钟视频之旅是您从未见到的飞机的部件的迷人洞察力，特别是在几十年的班车上。这次旅游是今年Virtual Def Con会议的一部分，美国最大的黑客会议。随着现代飞机依赖于更复杂的技术，安全研究人员对飞机如何防止乘客干扰航班时越来越感兴趣。
   在飞行中娱乐系统方面，安全性特别相关。网络安全教授去年发现了一个缓冲区溢流船上的英国航空公司航班。教授能够使用USB鼠标将长串文本输入到飞行中的聊天应用程序中，抵消座位的整个飞行中的娱乐系统。安全研究人员仍然狩猎漏洞，使他们能够与飞机部分公开可访问的地区与飞行系统沟通。
  对最新飞机的重点是安全性更重要。像波音的777x和787这样的现代飞机使用光纤网络，其中所有航空电子设备插入该网络，由运行飞行关键软件的一对计算机控制。它更像是您在办公楼内找到的传统网络，以及一些最新的客机甚至可以通过空中接收软件更新。不过，为现代飞机提供动力的软件并不总是可靠的。在软件故障后，波音刚刚恢复生产其陷入困境的737最大飞机，导致两个致命的崩溃，共有346名乘客和船员。
 尽管现代技术可用，但它并没有阻止软盘持续存在于其他行业。美国国防部只结束了10月份使用8英寸软盘，以协调国家的核动力，国际空间站充满了软盘。
note_en:
Boeing’s 747-400 aircraft, first introduced in 1988, is still receiving critical software updates through 3.5-inch floppy disks.   The Register reports that security researchers at Pen Test Partners recently got access to a British Airways 747, after the airline decided to retire its fleet following a plummet in travel during the coronavirus pandemic. The team was able to inspect the full avionics bay beneath the passenger deck, with its data center-like racks of modular black boxes that perform different functions for the plane.
 Pen Test Partners discovered a 3.5-inch floppy disk drive in the cockpit, which is used to load important navigation databases. It’s a database that has to be updated every 28 days, and an engineer visits each month with the latest updates.
  While it might sound surprising that 3.5-inch floppy disks are still in use on airplanes today, many of Boeing’s 737s have also been using floppy disks to load avionics software for years. The databases housed on these floppy discs are increasingly getting bigger, according to a 2015  report from Aviation Today. Some airlines have been moving away from the use of floppy discs, but others are stuck with engineers visiting each month to sit and load eight floppies with updates to airports, flight paths, runways, and more.
 The 10-minute video tour of the 747 (above) is a fascinating insight into the parts of the plane you never get to see, particularly on a decades-old airliner. The tour is part of this year’s virtual Def Con conference, the US’ largest hacker conference. As modern planes rely upon ever more sophisticated technology, security researchers are increasingly interested in how planes prevent passengers from interfering with flights.
   Security is particularly relevant when it comes to in-flight entertainment systems. A cybersecurity professor  discovered a buffer overflow exploit onboard a British Airways flight last year. The professor was able to use a USB mouse to input long strings of text into an in-flight chat app, crashing the entire in-flight entertainment system for his seat. Security researchers are still hunting for vulnerabilities that would allow them to communicate with flight systems from publicly accessible parts of planes.
  A focus on security is even more important on the latest aircraft. Modern planes like Boeing’s 777X and 787 use fiber networks, where all the avionics plug into this network and are controlled by a pair of computers that run flight critical software. It’s more of a traditional network like you’d find inside an office building, and some of the latest airliners even receive software updates over the air. The software that powers modern aircraft isn’t always reliable, though. Boeing only just  resumed production of its troubled 737 Max airplane after software glitches led to  two fatal crashes that killed a total of 346 passengers and crew members.
 Despite modern technology being available, it hasn’t stopped floppy disks from persisting in other industries. The US Defense Department only  ended the use of 8-inch floppy disks for coordinating the country’s nuclear forces in October, and the International Space Station is  full of floppy disks.
title: 波音747S仍然通过软盘获得关键更新
title_en: Boeing 747s still get critical updates via floppy disks
